title: Python final root command generation

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.hard_constraints: Keep scope to the remaining root workspace commands already present in cli_commands.json but absent from command_package_ir: system-intent, note-delegation-outcome, external-intent refresh-github, and prompt init/upgrade/uninstall.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Exact adapter ids, runtime handler helper names, focused conformance probes, and directly related checker hardening.
canonical_core.escalate_when: A correct fix requires package-resource operation IR redesign, non-Python target runtime policy changes, or unrelated module CLI behavior.
canonical_core.next_action: Add the remaining root command surfaces to command_package_ir and generated adapter metadata, wire thin runtime handlers, regenerate outputs, and prove there are no root compatibility fallback commands left.
proof_expectations: focused root help/probe commands for system-intent, note-delegation-outcome, external-intent, and prompt
touched_scope: tests/test_contract_tooling.py and focused workspace CLI tests when assertions need updating
completion_criteria: All executable root workspace CLI command surfaces in cli_commands.json are represented by command_package_ir generated commands.
Generated root parser/help includes system-intent, note-delegation-outcome, external-intent, and prompt without relying on compatibility parser ownership.
Runtime behavior remains hand-owned only behind _GENERATED_RUNTIME_HANDLERS thin adapters.
Generated package freshness, static proof, Python conformance, and focused CLI probes pass.
canonical_core.closeout_decision: pending until proof confirms the root compatibility fallback no longer owns visible Python CLI command surfaces

non_goals: Leave adjacent backlog or follow-on work out of this plan.

intent.constraints: Keep scope to the remaining root workspace command surfaces already present in cli_commands.json but absent from command_package_ir.
intent.latitude: Exact adapter ids, runtime helper names, focused conformance probes, and checker hardening directly needed by this slice.
intent.escalation: Escalate when a correct fix requires package-resource primitive redesign, non-Python target runtime policy changes, or unrelated module CLI behavior.

execution.next_step: Implement the final root workspace command generation slice and prove parser/dispatch ownership has moved to generated outputs.
execution.proof: Generated package freshness/static proof, Python conformance, and focused root CLI help/probe commands.

touched: tests/test_contract_tooling.py and focused workspace CLI tests when assertions need updating
invariants: Preserve the planning contract and keep the work bounded to this plan.

intent_continuity.this slice completes the larger intended: only if closeout proof also confirms no remaining Python CLI command surfaces are hand-owned outside generated dispatch

required_continuation.owner surface: command_package_ir python_cli_completion gate and python-generated-cli-completion-promotion lane
required_continuation.activation trigger: Any remaining hand-owned visible Python CLI surface, stale generated output, or failed completion proof.

iterative_follow_through.what this slice enabled: Final root workspace command surfaces can be tested as generated parser/dispatch entries.
iterative_follow_through.intentionally deferred: TypeScript semantic primitive parity, Bash, and PowerShell generated CLIs remain outside this Python branch.
iterative_follow_through.discovered implications: If generated command completeness checks do not detect fallback-only root commands, harden them in this slice.
iterative_follow_through.next likely slice: completion-promotion closeout only after this slice proves the remaining root commands are generated

intent_interpretation.chosen concrete what: Promote the four fallback-only root command groups into generated command_package_ir entries and runtime handler mappings.
intent_interpretation.review guidance: Confirm the scaffolded plan still matches the promoted item before broad implementation.

execution_bounds.allowed paths: Command contracts, generated command outputs, root runtime handler glue, targeted tests, and planning state for this execplan.
execution_bounds.max changed files: About 15 files; broaden only for checker hardening directly needed to prove no fallback-only root commands remain.
execution_bounds.required validation commands: generate_command_packages --check, check_generated_command_packages with Python conformance, focused root command help/probe commands, and targeted pytest if tests change.
execution_bounds.ask-before-refactor threshold: Ask before broadening beyond the promoted item.
execution_bounds.stop before touching: Unrelated backlog, adjacent modules, or canonical contracts not named by this plan.

stop_conditions.stop when: The work no longer matches the promoted item or its completion criteria.
stop_conditions.escalate when boundary reached: A correct fix requires changing the requested outcome or ownership boundary.
stop_conditions.escalate on scope drift: Implementation needs package-resource primitive redesign, non-Python target runtime policy changes, or unrelated module CLI behavior.
stop_conditions.escalate on proof failure: The selected proof cannot demonstrate the completion criteria.

context_budget.live working set: This execplan, python-generated-cli decomposition, command_package_ir, command_adapter_generation, python_operation_execution_inventory, _runtime_cli, generated outputs, and focused tests.
context_budget.recoverable later: Repo background, historical reviews, and deferred backlog unless compact outputs point there.
context_budget.externalize before shift: Update execution_run, proof_report, finished_run_review, and closeout_distillation before pausing.
context_budget.pre-work config pull: Use compact config/startup/summary outputs before opening raw planning or routing files.
context_budget.pre-work memory pull: Route to the narrowest relevant memory only when the task needs durable repo knowledge.
context_budget.tiny resumability note: Finish final fallback-only root command generation: system-intent, note-delegation-outcome, external-intent refresh-github, and prompt init/upgrade/uninstall.
context_budget.context-shift triggers: Proof failure, scope drift, interruption, handoff, or closeout.

post_decomposition_delegation.decision rule: After this slice is bounded, decide whether direct work, read-only exploration, implementation handoff, validation handoff, or stronger review improves quality or saves tokens safely.
post_decomposition_delegation.required evidence: slice id, route, reason, quality risk, token-saving class, read-first refs, write scope, proof burden, stop conditions, and return contract
post_decomposition_delegation.route skipped reason: The internal subagent tool requires an explicit current-turn delegation request, and this slice needs tightly coupled contract/runtime edits before a cheaper external handoff would be useful.

closure_check.accepted values: Accepted values: slice status = complete|completed|bounded slice complete; larger-intent status = closed|complete|completed for archive-and-close or open|partial|unfinished for archive-but-keep-lane-open; closure decision = archive-and-close|archive-but-keep-lane-open. Prefer `agentic-planning archive-plan <plan> --prepare-closeout` before hand-editing closeout fields.

improvement_signal_review.guidance: At closeout, separate acted-on, reported-only/routed, and dismissed incidental findings; keep this compact and evidence-backed.
